name: Johnny Smith
abstract: Johnny Henry Smith II (June 25, 1922 – June 11, 2013) was an American cool jazz and mainstream jazz guitarist. He wrote "Walk, Don't Run" in 1954.

concerts.label: Stan Kenton and His Orchestra: Festival of Modern American Jazz
Midnight Performance: Stan Kenton and His Orchestra: Festival of Modern American Jazz

about_quotes.speaker: Clayton Duerr
about_quotes.evidence: Like a Johnny Smith model, they have to maybe wait over a year for the ground thing.

about_quotes.speaker: Joe Bushkin
about_quotes.evidence: Johnny Smith played with us a few weeks at a time... Johnny Smith, Jake Hanna and Milt Hinton and myself, we were with Bing on tour.
about_quotes.doc_title: Joe Bushkin interviewed by Monk Rowe, Los Angeles, California, February 15, 1999

about_quotes.speaker: Louis Keppard
about_quotes.evidence: Of course I understand he hadn't spent it before that telegram. Gosh, I don't know that. Of course that was spent money. Well anyhow, then Lawrence Dewey, he got up there. Sugar County, of course they call him Johnny Smith.

about_quotes.speaker: Cliff Leeman
about_quotes.evidence: Well, there was a gentleman in my home town -- who Johnny Smith often speaks of, and I don't know whether he's alive or not, his name was Tom Robbins

about_quotes.speaker: George Benson
about_quotes.evidence: Organ trios had started to get popular because of Jimmy Smith and Johnny Smith and a few others... I was one of the few guys that played guitar.
about_quotes.doc_title: George Benson — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Bud Freeman
about_quotes.evidence: Johnny Smith, the guitarist who lives in Colorado... He's a fine guitarist and of course, Jake Hannah, and they're with Bing here.
